title: Lycianthes cladotrichota
taxonomy: Lycianthes cladotrichota is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae. This family includes over 3,000 species, commonly known as the nightshade family. It is native to South America, with a distribution that spans from the Andes to the subtropical regions. Lycianthes cladotrichota is a shrub-like plant that produces small white or purple flowers, and its classification is further divided into subcategories within the Solanaceae family. The classification of Lycianthes cladotrichota is a subject of ongoing research in the field of botany.

intro: Lycianthes cladotrichota, also known as the purple flowered lycianthes, is a plant species belonging to the Solanaceae family, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and gastritis.
